messages.subject: [OGRUG] seek advice/collaboration on how to promote R in our city
messages.subject_clean: [OGRUG] seek advice/collaboration on how to promote R in our city
messages.body_plain:
Hi dear fellow R users!

My name is Chunyun and I have been using R for the past three years, most
recently completed my PhD thesis using R. Long story short, I am writing to
you today to seek advice/collaboration on how to promote R in our city. I
recently designed and delivered a series of workshops on introduction to R.
My audience consisted of a professor from Carleton University and her lab
members. The experience really inspired me to consider repeating it again,
perhaps even on a larger scale. I looked up examples from other communities
and found a couple prominent ones such as the coding club
<https://ourcodingclub.github.io/> at University of Edinburgh and data
carpentry <https://datacarpentry.org/>.

If you are also excited by this short introduction and would like to chat
more, let's meet in person! I am currently working as a postdoc at Carleton
University and can meet you in the evenings on weekdays or during weekends.

Best wishes,  Chunyun

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [OGRUG] R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 44, Issue 1
messages.body_plain:
Paul Gilbert and I set up R-UG-Ottawa list and took over OGRUG from
Joseph Potvin a few years ago, intending to pass off to someone younger
and more active. So far that hasn't happened. Maybe it's time to think
of some meetup to try to kickstart the transition?

John Nash


On 2018-10-06 06:00 AM, r-ug-ottawa-request at r-project.org wrote:
> Send R-UG-Ottawa mailing list submissions to

> or, via email, send a message with subject or body 'help' to
> 	r-ug-ottawa-request at r-project.org
> 
> You can reach the person managing the list at

messages.subject: [OGRUG] seek advice/collaboration on how to promote R in our city (follow up)
messages.subject_clean: [OGRUG] seek advice/collaboration on how to promote R in our city (follow up)
messages.body_plain:
Hi dear fellow R users!

My name is Chunyun. About three weeks ago, I sent an email to this group seeking advice/collaboration on how to promote R in our city. I am resending this email just in case my last email didn't reach some of you.

I have been using R for the past three years, most recently completed my PhD thesis using R. This past summer, I designed and delivered a series of workshops on introduction to R. My audience consisted of a professor from Carleton University and her lab members. Most recently, I started a weekly R workshop with a small group of students at Carleton. These experiences really inspired me to wanting to connect with other people who are also interested in promoting R.

If you have experience with this subject and wouldn't mind sharing, let's meet in person! I am currently working as a postdoc at Carleton University and can meet you in the evenings on weekdays or during weekends.

Best wishes,  Chunyun

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
